   859→### 9.4 Rate Limiting
   860→
   861→| Consumer Type | Limit | Window | Scope |
   862→|---------------|-------|--------|-------|
   863→| Human users (authenticated) | 100 req | 1 min | Per user |
   864→| Verified bots / crawlers | 1000 req | 1 min | Per bot identity |
   865→
   866→Rate limits apply to all API endpoints. Exceeding limits returns `429 Too Many Requests` with `Retry-After` header.
   867→
   868→### 9.5 Rationale
   869→
   870→The LLM had generated mismatched class methods and variables from before and now. It had to be reprompted to recouncile the differences and create missing functions both here and in previous sections. Like mentioned before, it also had to be reprompted to standardize a ratelimiting policy here instead of arbitrary values elsewhere.
   871→
   872→The separation of public APIs, business logic, and data access layers is good practice in large systems, so I agree with the LLM's decisions here.

   954→          description: Channel not found
   955→        '429':
   956→          description: Rate limit exceeded
   957→
   958→components:
   959→  schemas:
   960→    ChannelVisibility:
   961→      type: string
   962→      enum: [PUBLIC_INDEXABLE, PUBLIC_NO_INDEX, PRIVATE]
   963→
   964→    VisibilityUpdateRequest:
   965→      type: object
   966→      required: [visibility]
   967→      properties:
   968→        visibility:
   969→          $ref: '#/components/schemas/ChannelVisibility'
   970→
   971→    VisibilityUpdateResponse:
   972→      type: object
   973→      properties:
   974→        success:
   975→          type: boolean
   976→        channel:
   977→          $ref: '#/components/schemas/ChannelDTO'
   978→        previousVisibility:
   979→          $ref: '#/components/schemas/ChannelVisibility'
   980→        indexingStatus:
   981→          type: string
   982→          enum: [PENDING, INDEXED, NOT_INDEXED, REMOVAL_REQUESTED]
   983→```
   984→
   985→### 10.3 Cross-Spec Event Integration
   986→
   987→When `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` is emitted:
   988→
   989→| New Visibility | Downstream Action (SEO Spec) | Downstream Action (Guest View Spec) |
   990→|---------------|------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
   991→| `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE` | Generate meta tags for channel | Warm guest view cache |
   992→| `PUBLIC_NO_INDEX` | Update meta tags (add noindex) | Keep guest view cache (public content) |
   993→| `PRIVATE` | Delete meta tags for channel | Invalidate guest view cache |
   994→
   995→### 10.4 Rationale
   996→
   997→The LLM correctly generated the public API specification. It did not need to be reprompted for any fixes here. The generated API specification is correct and exposes the endpoints necessary for this specific user story. 
   998→
   999→Significant inconsistencies in classes existed between sections 2, 3, 9, and 10, so a verification pass was necessary after the document was generated to fix them.

   650→### 4.10 Rationale
   651→
   652→The list of classes clearly states the moving parts for ensuring guest user can access public channels and their messages, with handling caching. The classes cover all the responsibilities needed for this feature to function from route handling to retrieving the public data to formatting the response to the guest. The inclusion of caching and bot detection justified since retrieval of the same content from multiple guest is unnecessary more work on the server.

   656→## 5. State Diagrams
   657→
   658→### 5.1 System State Variables
   659→
   660→| Variable | Type | Description |
   661→|----------|------|-------------|
   662→| request.path | string | Current URL path being requested |
   663→| channel.visibility | ChannelVisibility | Visibility state of requested channel |
   664→| cache.status | CacheStatus | Whether response is cached (HIT/MISS/STALE) |
   665→| guest.sessionId | string | Anonymous session identifier |
   666→| page.loadState | LoadState | Current page loading state |
   667→| messages.pagination | PaginationState | Current pagination position |
   668→
   669→> **Convention:** `is_public` (boolean) applies to **servers** — whether the server appears in discovery. `visibility` (enum: `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE`, `PUBLIC_NO_INDEX`, `PRIVATE`) applies to **channels** — whether channel content is accessible to guests and/or indexed by search engines.
   670→
   671→### 5.2 Page Load State Machine
   672→
   673→```
   674→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   675→│                              LEGEND                                              │
   676→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   677→│  (( ))  Initial State        [ ]  State         < >  Decision                   │
   678→│  ─────► Transition           [[ ]] Final State                                  │
   679→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   680→
   681→                         (( S0: URL Requested ))
   682→                                    │
   683→                                    │ GET /c/{server}/{channel}
   684→                                    ▼
   685→                    ┌───────────────────────────────┐
   686→                    │ S1: Edge Cache Check          │
   687→                    │ ───────────────────────────── │
   688→                    │ cache.status = CHECKING       │
   689→                    │ request.path = /c/srv/ch      │
   690→                    └───────────────┬───────────────┘
   691→                                    │
   692→                            < Cache Hit? >
   693→                           /              \
   694→                          / Yes            \ No (MISS)
   695→                         ▼                  ▼
   696→        ┌─────────────────────────┐    ┌───────────────────────────────┐
   697→        │ S2: Serve Cached        │    │ S3: Origin Request            │
   698→        │ ─────────────────────── │    │ ───────────────────────────── │
   699→        │ cache.status = HIT      │    │ cache.status = MISS           │
   700→        │ response.source = EDGE  │    │ request.forwarded = true      │
   701→        └───────────┬─────────────┘    └───────────────┬───────────────┘
   702→                    │                                  │
   703→                    │                                  ▼
   704→                    │                  ┌───────────────────────────────┐
   705→                    │                  │ S4: Visibility Check          │
   706→                    │                  │ ───────────────────────────── │
   707→                    │                  │ channel.visibility = ?        │
   708→                    │                  └───────────────┬───────────────┘
   709→                    │                                  │
   710→                    │                  ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐
   711→                    │                  │                               │
   712→                    │          < Is Public? >                          │
   713→                    │         /               \                        │
   714→                    │        / No              \ Yes                   │
   715→                    │       ▼                   ▼                      │
   716→                    │  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌───────────────────────────────┐
   717→                    │  │ S5: Access      │  │ S6: Fetch Content             │
   718→                    │  │ Denied          │  │ ───────────────────────────── │
   719→                    │  │ ─────────────── │  │ messages = loading            │
   720→                    │  │ error = 403     │  │ server = loading              │
   721→                    │  │ OR              │  │ channel = loading             │
   722→                    │  │ redirect = true │  └───────────────┬───────────────┘
   723→                    │  └────────┬────────┘                  │
   724→                    │           │                           ▼
   725→                    │           │          ┌───────────────────────────────┐
   726→                    │           │          │ S7: Render Page               │
   727→                    │           │          │ ───────────────────────────── │
   728→                    │           │          │ page.loadState = COMPLETE     │
   729→                    │           │          │ messages = MessageDTO[]       │
   730→                    │           │          │ seo.tags = generated          │
   731→                    │           │          └───────────────┬───────────────┘
   732→                    │           │                          │
   733→                    │           │                          ▼
   734→                    │           │          ┌───────────────────────────────┐
   735→                    │           │          │ S8: Cache Response            │
   736→                    │           │          │ ───────────────────────────── │
   737→                    │           │          │ cache.stored = true           │
   738→                    │           │          │ cache.ttl = 60s               │
   739→                    │           │          └───────────────┬───────────────┘
   740→                    │           │                          │
   741→                    └───────────┴──────────────────────────┘
   742→                                           │
   743→                                           ▼
   744→                         [[ S9: Response Delivered ]]
   745→                         ─────────────────────────────
   746→                         page.loadState = DELIVERED
   747→                         guest can view content
   748→
   749→
   750→State Transition Table:
   751→┌────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
   752→│ Current State      │ Condition/Action           │ Next State         │ Side Effects                 │
   753→├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────┤
   754→│ S1: Cache Check    │ Cache key exists, valid    │ S2: Serve Cached   │ Return cached HTML           │
   755→│ S1: Cache Check    │ Cache stale (expired <300s)│ S2: Serve Cached   │ Return stale HTML; trigger   │
   756→│                    │                            │                    │ background revalidation      │
   757→│ S1: Cache Check    │ Cache miss or expired      │ S3: Origin Request │ Forward to origin            │
   758→│ S3: Origin Request │ Always                     │ S4: Visibility     │ Query database               │
   759→│ S4: Visibility     │ visibility != PUBLIC_*     │ S5: Access Denied  │ Return 403 or redirect       │
   760→│ S4: Visibility     │ visibility = PUBLIC_*      │ S6: Fetch Content  │ Query messages               │
   761→│ S6: Fetch Content  │ Content retrieved          │ S7: Render Page    │ Generate HTML                │
   762→│ S7: Render Page    │ Rendering complete         │ S8: Cache Response │ Store in edge cache          │
   763→│ S2, S8             │ Response ready             │ S9: Delivered      │ Send to client               │
   764→└────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
   765→```

   813→### 5.4 Access Denial State Machine
   814→
   815→```
   816→                         (( D0: Private Channel Requested ))
   817→                                    │
   818→                                    │ visibility = PRIVATE
   819→                                    ▼
   820→                    ┌───────────────────────────────┐
   821→                    │ D1: Evaluate Response         │
   822→                    │ ───────────────────────────── │
   823→                    │ server.isPublic = ?           │
   824→                    │ referrer.source = ?           │
   825→                    └───────────────┬───────────────┘
   826→                                    │
   827→                    ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
   828→                    │               │               │
   829→            < From search? >  < Server public? >   │
   830→                   │               │               │
   831→                   ▼               ▼               ▼
   832→    ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐
   833→    │ D2: Show Login   │  │ D3: Show Server  │  │ D4: Show 404     │
   834→    │ Prompt           │  │ Landing          │  │ Not Found        │
   835→    │ ──────────────── │  │ ──────────────── │  │ ──────────────── │
   836→    │ "Log in to view  │  │ Redirect to      │  │ Channel does not │
   837→    │ this channel"    │  │ /s/{server}      │  │ exist or is      │
   838→    │ + explain why    │  │ Show public      │  │ private          │
   839→    │ + link to join   │  │ channels list    │  │ (no info leak)   │
   840→    └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘
   841→
   842→Decision Logic:
   843→┌────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┐
   844→│ Condition              │ Response               │ Rationale              │
   845→├────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────┤
   846→│ Channel doesn't exist  │ 404 Not Found          │ Don't reveal existence │
   847→│ Channel private,       │ 403 + Login prompt     │ User expected content  │
   848→│   from search          │                        │                        │
   849→│ Channel private,       │ Redirect to server     │ Show available content │
   850→│   server is public     │   landing              │                        │
   851→│ Channel private,       │ 404 Not Found          │ Don't reveal existence │
   852→│   server is private    │                        │                        │
   853→└────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┴────────────────────────┘
   854→```
   855→
   856→### 5.5 Rationale
   857→
   858→These states were chosen to show the phases a guest can be for viewing a public channel, the states handle critical edge cases a guest can experience since the endpoints are publicily accessible such as trying to visit a private channel or channel that isn't cached. The state also has no login redirect due to the fact that this feature is supposed allow anonymous users to access public channels. Importantly each state has a clear end to each phase so the guest ins't stuck in a loop state that they can't get out off.
   859→
   860→---

  1217→### 6.4 Scenario: Search Engine Bot Crawls Public Channel
  1218→
  1219→**Scenario Description:** A search engine bot (Googlebot, Bingbot, etc.) crawls a public channel page. The system serves optimized content with appropriate SEO signals.
  1220→
  1221→```
  1222→    (( START: Bot requests public channel ))
  1223→    User-Agent: Googlebot/2.1
  1224→    URL: https://harmony.app/c/opensource/announcements
  1225→                            │
  1226→                            ▼

  1235→            │ [F4.2] Apply bot-specific     │
  1236→            │ handling                      │
  1237→            │ - Skip JS-dependent content   │
  1238→            │ - Ensure full HTML render     │
  1239→            │ - Apply bot rate limits       │
  1240→            └───────────────┬───────────────┘
  1241→                            │
  1242→                            ▼
  1243→            (Same visibility and content flow as F1.4-F1.18)
  1244→                            │
  1245→                            ▼
  1246→            ┌───────────────────────────────┐

  1248→            │ response                      │
  1249→            │                               │
  1250→            │ Include:                      │
  1251→            │ - Full message content inline │
  1252→            │ - Structured data (JSON-LD)   │
  1253→            │ - Canonical URL               │
  1254→            │ - Breadcrumb schema           │
  1255→            │ - hreflang tags (if i18n)     │
  1256→            └───────────────┬───────────────┘
  1257→                            │
  1258→                            ▼

  1262→            │ X-Robots-Tag: index, follow   │
  1263→            │ Link: <canonical>; rel=canon  │

  1292→
  1293→### 6.5 Cross-Spec Integration: VISIBILITY_CHANGED Event Consumption
  1294→
  1295→When the Channel Visibility Toggle spec emits a `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event (via Redis Pub/Sub EventBus), the Guest Public Channel View system reacts as follows:
  1296→
  1297→| New Visibility | Guest View Action |
  1298→|---------------|-------------------|
  1299→| `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE` | Warm guest view cache for channel; begin serving public content |
  1300→| `PUBLIC_NO_INDEX` | Keep guest view cache (content still public); update `X-Robots-Tag` to `noindex` |
  1301→| `PRIVATE` | Invalidate all guest view caches for channel; return 403/404 on subsequent requests |
  1302→
  1303→**Event Payload Consumed:**
  1304→```typescript
  1305→interface VisibilityChangeEvent {
  1306→  channelId: string;        // UUID
  1307→  oldVisibility: ChannelVisibility;
  1308→  newVisibility: ChannelVisibility;
  1309→  actorId: string;          // UUID of admin who made the change
  1310→  timestamp: DateTime;
  1311→}
  1312→```
  1313→
  1314→**Cache Keys Invalidated on PRIVATE:**
  1315→- `channel:{channelId}:visibility`
  1316→- `channel:{channelId}:msgs:*` (all pages)
  1317→- `server:{serverId}:info`
  1318→
  1319→### 6.6 Rationale 
  1320→
  1321→The flow charts depict the major flow cases a guest will experience for Harmony. The first flow covers the primary case that the guest visits the public channel from search engine result, which is the primary aim for Harmony, to be able to provide public channel information to guests without the need of logging in. The second flow covers the edge case a guests tries to visit a private channel, safely redirecting them without revealing any sensitive information about the server. The third flow covers the guest loading more messages of the channel, allowing the guest to infinitely scroll older messages. The fourth flow covers the public channels to be crawlable by search engine bots, so guests searching for information related to public channels can see it. 
  1322→
  1323→---

  1325→## 7. Development Risks and Failures
  1326→
  1327→### 7.1 Runtime Failures
  1328→
  1329→| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1330→|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1331→| RF-1 | SSR render crash | 500 error page | React hydration failure | Error boundary catches; shows fallback UI | Medium | High |
  1332→| RF-2 | Database query timeout | Slow page load or timeout | Connection pool exhaustion | Query optimization; read replicas; timeout handling | Medium | Medium |
  1333→| RF-3 | Cache corruption | Stale or incorrect content shown | Cache-DB inconsistency | Cache invalidation; serve from DB | Low | Medium |
  1334→| RF-4 | Memory leak in SSR | Gradual performance degradation | Node.js process OOM | Process recycling; memory monitoring | Low | High |
  1335→| RF-5 | Infinite scroll breaks | Users can't load more messages | Client JS error | Fallback pagination links; error logging | Medium | Low |
  1336→| RF-6 | SEO data generation fails | Missing meta tags | Empty title/description | Default fallback values; monitoring | Low | Medium |
  1337→| RF-7 | Content filter regex timeout | Slow response on large messages | CPU spike | Regex timeout limits; message size limits | Low | Medium |
  1338→
  1339→### 7.2 Connectivity Failures
  1340→
  1341→| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1342→|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1343→| CF-1 | CDN edge outage | Regional unavailability | Cache layer bypassed | Multi-CDN failover; origin direct access | Low | High |
  1344→| CF-2 | Origin unreachable | 502/503 errors | CDN can't reach server | Health checks; auto-scaling; stale-while-revalidate | Low | High |
  1345→| CF-3 | Database connection loss | 500 errors | All queries fail | Connection retry; replica failover | Low | Critical |
  1346→| CF-4 | Redis cache unavailable | Slower responses | Cache misses; DB load increase | Degrade gracefully; serve from DB | Medium | Medium |
  1347→| CF-5 | Slow network to guest | Long load times | Time to first byte high | CDN edge caching; image optimization | Medium | Medium |
  1348→
  1349→### 7.3 Hardware Failures
  1350→
  1351→| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1352→|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1353→| HF-1 | Web server crash | Brief unavailability | Container restart | Auto-restart; load balancer rerouting | Low | Medium |
  1354→| HF-2 | Database server down | Complete outage | All writes/reads fail | Automatic failover to replica | Very Low | Critical |
  1355→| HF-3 | Storage full | New messages not visible | Write failures | Storage alerts; auto-scaling storage | Low | High |
  1356→
  1357→### 7.4 Security/Intruder Failures
  1358→
  1359→| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1360→|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1361→| IF-1 | DDoS on public pages | Service degradation | Resource exhaustion | CloudFlare DDoS protection; rate limiting | Medium | High |
  1362→| IF-2 | Scraping abuse | None (internal cost) | Bandwidth/compute abuse | Bot detection; rate limiting; CAPTCHA | High | Medium |
  1363→| IF-3 | Visibility bypass attempt | 403 error (if working) | Security log entry | Input validation; server-side checks | Medium | Critical |
  1364→| IF-4 | XSS via message content | Malicious script execution | User session compromise | Content sanitization; CSP headers | Low | Critical |
  1365→| IF-5 | Cache poisoning | Wrong content served | Cache serves malicious response | Cache key validation; purge capability | Very Low | Critical |
  1366→| IF-6 | Enumeration attack | None (if protected) | Load on database | Rate limiting; no existence disclosure | Medium | Low |
  1367→
  1368→### 7.5 Content/Data Failures
  1369→
  1370→| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1371→|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1372→| DF-1 | Private content exposed | Privacy breach | Visibility check bypassed | Audit; immediate visibility fix; notification | Very Low | Critical |
  1373→| DF-2 | User ID leaked in public DTO | Privacy concern | PII exposure | DTO review; immediate patch | Low | High |
  1374→| DF-3 | Deleted message shown | Confusing content | Soft delete not respected | Query filter fix; cache purge | Low | Medium |
  1375→| DF-4 | Attachment not loading | Broken images/files | Storage access issue | CDN retry; fallback placeholder | Medium | Low |
  1376→
  1377→### 7.6 Failure Priority Matrix
  1378→
  1379→```
  1380→                    Impact
  1381→                    Low         Medium      High        Critical
  1382→            ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  1383→     High   │ IF-2      │           │ IF-1      │              │
  1384→            ├───────────┼───────────┼───────────┼──────────────┤
  1385→            │ RF-5      │ RF-2,CF-4 │ RF-1      │              │
  1386→   Medium   │ IF-6      │ CF-5      │           │ IF-3         │
  1387→            ├───────────┼───────────┼───────────┼──────────────┤
  1388→            │           │ RF-3,RF-6 │ RF-4,HF-1 │ CF-3,IF-4    │
  1389→     Low    │ DF-4      │ RF-7,DF-3 │ DF-2,HF-3 │ DF-1         │
  1390→            ├───────────┼───────────┼───────────┼──────────────┤
  1391→  Very Low  │           │           │ CF-1,CF-2 │ HF-2,IF-5    │
  1392→            └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  1393→```
  1394→
  1395→### 7.7 Rationale 
  1396→
  1397→The development risks and failures categories were chosen to represent the primary threat this feature can face. The runtime and connectivity failures are catagorized due to the feature being a publicly accessible endpoint that any guest can use, leading to unpredictable traffic volumes. Intruder risks face the highest priorty due to the endpoint having no authentication since guests aren't required to log in.

  1406→| T2 | React | 18.2+ | UI framework | Component model; hydration support | https://react.dev/ |

  1411→| T7 | Prisma | 5.8+ | ORM | Type-safe database access | https://www.prisma.io/ |
  1412→| T8 | tRPC | 10.45+ | End-to-end typesafe APIs (authenticated internal) | Type-safe client-server communication | https://trpc.io/ |

  1414→| T10 | TailwindCSS | 3.4+ | Styling | Utility-first; consistent design | https://tailwindcss.com/ |
  1415→| T11 | CloudFlare | N/A | CDN/Edge | Global caching; DDoS protection; edge workers | https://www.cloudflare.com/ |

  1418→| T14 | Bing Webmaster API | v1 | Microsoft search integration | URL submission; sitemap ping | https://www.bing.com/webmasters |
  1419→| T15 | Jest | 29+ | Unit testing | Component and service tests | https://jestjs.io/ |
  1420→| T16 | Playwright | 1.40+ | E2E testing | SEO verification; crawl simulation | https://playwright.dev/ |
  1421→| T17 | sanitize-html | 2.12+ | HTML sanitization | XSS prevention (Node.js-native) | https://github.com/apostrophecms/sanitize-html |
  1422→| T18 | schema-dts | 1.1+ | Structured data types | Type-safe JSON-LD generation | https://github.com/google/schema-dts |
  1423→| T19 | intersection-observer | (polyfill) | Infinite scroll | Cross-browser scroll detection | https://github.com/w3c/IntersectionObserver |
  1424→| T20 | sharp | 0.33+ | Image processing | Thumbnail generation; optimization | https://sharp.pixelplumbing.com/ |
  1425→| T21 | Lighthouse CI | 11+ | Performance testing | Core Web Vitals monitoring | https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse-ci |
  1426→
  1427→> **Convention:** tRPC is used for authenticated internal APIs between client and server. Public-facing endpoints (public channel pages, sitemaps, robots.txt) use REST for maximum compatibility with crawlers and third-party consumers.
  1428→
  1429→### 8.1 EventBus
  1430→
  1431→**Technology:** Redis Pub/Sub (T6)
  1432→
  1433→Event types consumed by this spec:
  1434→
  1435→| Event | Source Spec | Description |
  1436→|-------|-------------|-------------|
  1437→| `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` | Channel Visibility Toggle | Channel visibility state changed; invalidate/warm caches |
  1438→| `MESSAGE_CREATED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | New message in public channel; invalidate message cache |
  1439→| `MESSAGE_EDITED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message edited; invalidate affected cache pages |
  1440→| `MESSAGE_DELETED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message deleted; invalidate affected cache pages |
  1441→
  1442→### 8.2 Rationale
  1443→
  1444→The technology stack was chosen to align with Harmony's architecture design and meet the needs of this feature. The primary langauge to be used for Harmony is Typescript, the reason is ensuring type safety accross the website, reducing runtime errors. Redis will serve our caching layer, for fast reads and session storage. Next.js was selected for its out of the box tools and capabilities it provide for Harmony such as SSR.

  1818→### 9.5 Rate Limiting
  1819→
  1820→| Consumer Type | Limit | Window | Enforcement |
  1821→|--------------|-------|--------|-------------|
  1822→| Human (anonymous) | 100 requests | 1 minute | Token bucket per IP |
  1823→| Verified bot (Googlebot, Bingbot, etc.) | 1000 requests | 1 minute | User-Agent verification |
  1824→| Suspicious pattern | CAPTCHA challenge | After 500 page views/hour | Behavioral analysis |
  1825→
  1826→### 9.6 Pagination Precedence
  1827→
  1828→When both cursor and page parameters are provided, cursor-based pagination takes precedence:
  1829→
  1830→1. If `before` or `after` (cursor) is provided, use cursor-based pagination (ignore `page`)
  1831→2. Otherwise, fall back to offset-based pagination using `page` and `limit`
  1832→3. Default: `page=1`, `limit=50`
  1833→
  1834→### Rationale
  1835→
  1836→The APIs for Guest Public Channel View, clearly handle the usage of the feature for being able to publicily access channels that are from the original search message. The first section APIs' purpose is for handling showing the information for the channel in which was derived from the search query and being able to load further messages in the channel. Next section APIs' purpose is for handling the server information once there, such as showing the server information and other public channels inside that server.
  1837→

  2166→
  2167→### 10.3 Cross-Spec Event Integration
  2168→
  2169→When `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` is emitted by the Channel Visibility Toggle spec:
  2170→
  2171→| New Visibility | Downstream Action (Guest View Spec) |
  2172→|---------------|--------------------------------------|
  2173→| `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE` | Warm guest view cache for channel |
  2174→| `PUBLIC_NO_INDEX` | Keep guest view cache (public content, but update X-Robots-Tag) |
  2175→| `PRIVATE` | Invalidate guest view cache; return 403/404 |
  2176→
  2177→### 10.4 Rationale 
  2178→
  2179→The public interfaces categories appropriately define the public method this featur needs for other modules to intercat with. For the public api, the public method serve its purpose for providing the necessary entry points other modules need to allow guests to view public channels without logging in. The access controls purpose is to protect private channels from being accessed by guests, verifying that the channel is public. Content delivery and data access purpose is guest receiving the public information the channel has.

1→# Unified Backend Architecture — Harmony 2→ 3→> **Scope:** This document specifies the shared backend that powers all three P3 features—**Channel Visibility Toggle**, **Guest Public Channel View**, and **SEO Meta Tag Generation**—in a single, cohesive service layer. It is the authoritative reference for module boundaries, data models, APIs, and class ownership. 4→ 5→--- 6→ 7→## 1. Design Rationale 8→ 9→### 1.1 Why a Unified Backend? 10→ 11→Each feature spec was authored independently and defines its own modules, classes, and schemas. Left unmerged, the codebase would contain three competing `ChannelRepository` classes, duplicate cache logic, and inconsistent database schemas. A unified backend eliminates this redundancy while preserving each feature's domain-specific logic. 12→ 13→### 1.2 Key Design Choices 14→ 15→| Decision | Choice | Justification | 16→|----------|--------|---------------| 17→| **Primary Language** | TypeScript 5.3+ | End-to-end type safety (client + server); single language reduces context-switching. | 18→| **Database** | PostgreSQL 16+ | ACID guarantees for visibility state transitions; native `ENUM` types for visibility; `JSONB` for flexible audit payloads; partial indexes for efficient public-channel queries. | 19→| **Cache / EventBus** | Redis 7.2+ | Sub-millisecond reads for visibility checks on every public page load; Pub/Sub for cross-module event propagation (`VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_CREATED`, etc.) without tight coupling. | 20→| **Authenticated APIs** | tRPC 11 | End-to-end type inference between Next.js client and Express server; eliminates hand-written API clients for admin operations. | 21→| **Public APIs** | REST (Express) | Search-engine crawlers, social-media link unfurlers, and external consumers require plain HTTP. tRPC's binary protocol is invisible to these consumers. | 22→| **Real-time Transport** | SSE (Server-Sent Events) | Native browser `EventSource` requires no client library; unidirectional server→client push fits message delivery perfectly; works over existing HTTP infrastructure with no WebSocket upgrade negotiation. | 23→| **ORM** | Prisma 5.8+ | Type-safe schema definitions; auto-generated migrations; integrates with PostgreSQL enums. | 24→| **Runtime Validation** | Zod 3.22+ | Composes with tRPC for automatic request/response validation; shared between client and server. | 25→| **SSR Framework** | Next.js 14+ | Server-side rendering is critical for SEO; server components reduce client bundle for public pages. | 26→| **HTML Sanitization** | sanitize-html 2.12+ | XSS prevention for user-generated content rendered on public pages. Node.js-native (no DOM dependency). | 27→ 28→### 1.3 tRPC + REST Split 29→ 30→``` 31→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ 32→│ API Surface │ 33→├──────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┤ 34→│ tRPC (Authenticated)│ REST (Public/Unauthenticated) │ 35→│ │ │ 36→│ • Channel settings │ • GET /c/{server}/{channel} (SSR page) │ 37→│ • Visibility toggle │ • GET /api/public/channels/… (messages) │ 38→│ • Audit log queries │ • GET /api/public/servers/… (server) │ 39→│ • Admin meta-tag │ • GET /sitemap/{server}.xml │ 40→│ overrides │ • GET /robots.txt │ 41→│ │ • GET /s/{server} (server landing) │ 42→└──────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────┘ 43→``` 44→ 45→**Why the split?** Crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot) and social-media unfurlers (Facebook, Twitter/X, Slack) make standard HTTP requests. They cannot consume tRPC. Admin operations (visibility toggling, meta-tag overrides) benefit from tRPC's type inference and are only used by authenticated Harmony clients. 46→ 47→--- 48→ 49→## 2. System Architecture Overview 50→ 51→### 2.1 High-Level Architecture Diagram 52→ 53→```mermaid 54→graph TB 55→ subgraph External["External Actors"] 56→ Admin["🔑 Admin User"] 57→ Guest["👤 Guest User"] 58→ Bot["🤖 Search Engine Bot"] 59→ end 60→ 61→ subgraph Client["Client Layer — Next.js"] 62→ AdminUI["Admin Dashboard
(M-CV1)"] 63→ PublicUI["Public View SSR
(M-GV1)"] 64→ ClientInt["Client Interaction
(M-GV2)"] 65→ end 66→ 67→ subgraph Server["Server Layer — Express + tRPC"] 68→ APIGateway["API Gateway
(M-B1)"] 69→ AccessCtrl["Access Control
(M-B2)"] 70→ VisBiz["Visibility Service
(M-B3)"] 71→ ContentDel["Content Delivery

(M-B5)"] 73→ SEOIndex["SEO & Indexing
(M-B6)"] 74→ BgProcess["Background Workers
(M-B7)"] 75→ end 76→ 77→ subgraph Data["Data Layer"] 78→ PG[("PostgreSQL")] 79→ Redis[("Redis
Cache + Pub/Sub")] 80→ end 81→ 82→ subgraph ExtSystems["External Systems"] 83→ Google["Google Search Console"] 84→ Bing["Bing Webmaster API"] 85→ end 86→ 87→ Admin -->|tRPC| AdminUI 88→ Guest -->|HTTPS| PublicUI 89→ Bot -->|HTTPS| PublicUI 90→ AdminUI -->|tRPC| APIGateway 91→ PublicUI -->|Internal| APIGateway 92→ ClientInt -->|REST| APIGateway 93→ APIGateway --> AccessCtrl 94→ APIGateway --> VisBiz 95→ APIGateway --> ContentDel 96→ APIGateway --> MetaTag 97→ AccessCtrl --> VisBiz 98→ VisBiz --> SEOIndex 99→ ContentDel --> MetaTag 100→ SEOIndex --> BgProcess Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. ```mermaid
classDiagram
    class IVisibilityToggle {
        <<interface>>
        +setVisibility()
        +getVisibility()
        +canChangeVisibility()
    }

    class ChannelVisibilityService {
        -channelRepository
        -auditLogger
        -eventBus
        -permissionService
        +setVisibility()
        +getVisibility()
        +canChangeVisibility()
        -validateTransition()
        -emitVisibilityChange()
    }

    class ChannelRepository {
        -database
        -cache
        +findById()
        +findBySlug()
        +update()
        +findPublicByServerId()
        +getVisibility()
        +getMetadata()
        -invalidateCache()
        -getCacheKey()
    }

    class AuditLogService {
        +logVisibilityChange()
        +getAuditHistory()
        +exportAuditLog()
    }

    class PermissionService {
        +canManageChannel()
        +isServerAdmin()
        +getEffectivePermissions()
    }

    class Channel {
        <<Entity>>
        +id UUID
        +serverId UUID
        +name string
        +slug string
        +visibility Enum
        +topic string
        +position number
        +indexedAt DateTime
        +createdAt DateTime
        +updatedAt DateTime
        +isPublic()
        +isIndexable()
        +setVisibility()
    }

    class AuditLogEntry {
        <<Entity>>
        +id UUID
        +channelId UUID
        +actorId UUID
        +action string
        +oldValue JSON
        +newValue JSON
        +timestamp DateTime
        +ipAddress string
    }

    class VisibilityChangeEvent {
        <<Event>>
        +channelId UUID
        +oldVisibility Enum
        +newVisibility Enum
        +actorId UUID
        +timestamp DateTime
    }

    class IndexingService {
        -sitemapGenerator
        -searchEngineNotifier
        +updateSitemap()
        +notifySearchEngines()
        +generateCanonicalUrl()
        +getRobotsDirectives()
    }

    class SitemapGenerator {
        -publicChannelRepo
        +generate()
        +getLastModified()
    }

    IVisibilityToggle <|.. ChannelVisibilityService
    ChannelVisibilityService o-- ChannelRepository
    ChannelVisibilityService o-- AuditLogService
    ChannelVisibilityService o-- PermissionService
    ChannelRepository *-- Channel
    AuditLogService ..> AuditLogEntry
    ChannelVisibilityService ..> VisibilityChangeEvent
    IndexingService --> SitemapGenerator
```

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> S1 : GET /c/{server}/{channel}

    S1 : S1: Edge Cache Check\ncache.status=CHECKING
    S2 : S2: Serve Cached\ncache.status=HIT\nresponse.source=EDGE
    S3 : S3: Origin Request\ncache.status=MISS\nrequest.forwarded=true
    S4 : S4: Visibility Check\nchannel.visibility=?
    S5 : S5: Access Denied\nerror=403 OR redirect=true
    S6 : S6: Fetch Content\nmessages=loading\nserver=loading
    S7 : S7: Render Page\npage.loadState=COMPLETE\nseo.tags=generated
    S8 : S8: Cache Response\ncache.stored=true\ncache.ttl=60s
    S9 : S9: Response Delivered\npage.loadState=DELIVERED

    S1 --> S2 : Cache hit (valid or stale <300s)\n[return cached HTML; stale triggers background revalidation]
    S1 --> S3 : Cache miss or expired\n[forward to origin]
    S3 --> S4 : Always\n[query database]
    S4 --> S5 : visibility != PUBLIC_*\n[return 403 or redirect]
    S4 --> S6 : visibility = PUBLIC_*\n[query messages]
    S6 --> S7 : Content retrieved\n[generate HTML]
    S7 --> S8 : Rendering complete\n[store in edge cache]
    S2 --> S9 : Response ready\n[send to client]
    S8 --> S9 : Response ready\n[send to client]
    S5 --> S9 : Response ready\n[send to client]
```

19-| **Cache / EventBus** | Redis 7.2+ | Sub-millisecond reads for visibility checks on every public page load; Pub/Sub for cross-module event propagation (`VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_CREATED`, etc.) without tight coupling. |
20-| **Authenticated APIs** | tRPC 11 | End-to-end type inference between Next.js client and Express server; eliminates hand-written API clients for admin operations. |
21:| **Public APIs** | REST (Express) | Search-engine crawlers, social-media link unfurlers, and external consumers require plain HTTP. tRPC's binary protocol is invisible to these consumers. |
22-| **Real-time Transport** | SSE (Server-Sent Events) | Native browser `EventSource` requires no client library; unidirectional server→client push fits message delivery perfectly; works over existing HTTP infrastructure with no WebSocket upgrade negotiation. |
23-| **ORM** | Prisma 5.8+ | Type-safe schema definitions; auto-generated migrations; integrates with PostgreSQL enums. |
--
26-| **HTML Sanitization** | sanitize-html 2.12+ | XSS prevention for user-generated content rendered on public pages. Node.js-native (no DOM dependency). |

    subgraph MB4["M-B4: Content Delivery"]
        MessageService["MessageService\ngetMessagesForPublicView()\ngetMessageById()"]
        AuthorService["AuthorService\ngetPublicAuthorInfo()\nanonymizeAuthor()"]
        AttachmentService["AttachmentService\ngetPublicAttachmentUrl()\nisAttachmentPublic()"]
        SEOService["SEOService\ngeneratePageTitle()\ngenerateDescription()\ngenerateStructuredData()"]
    end

    subgraph MD1["M-D1: Data Access"]
        ChannelRepo["ChannelRepository\nfindBySlug()\nfindPublicByServerId()\ngetVisibility()"]
        MessageRepo["MessageRepository\nfindByChannelPaginated()\nfindById()"]
        ServerRepo["ServerRepository\nfindBySlug()\ngetPublicInfo()"]
        UserRepo["UserRepository\nfindById()\ngetPublicProfile()"]
    end

    subgraph MD2["M-D2: Persistence (PostgreSQL)"]
        ServersTable[("servers")]
        ChannelsTable[("channels")]
        MessagesTable[("messages")]
        UsersTable[("users")]
        AttachmentsTable[("attachments")]
    end

    subgraph MD3["M-D3: Cache (Redis)"]
        VisCache["channel:{channelId}:visibility\nTTL: 3600s (M-B3 owner)"]
        MsgsCache["channel:msgs:{channelId}:page:{pageNum}\nTTL: 60s (M-B4 owner)"]
        ServerCache["server:{serverId}:info\nTTL: 300s (M-B4 owner)"]
        GuestSession["guest:session:{sessionId}\nTTL: 86400s (M-B2 owner)"]
    end

| Label | Class Name | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------------|------|---------|
| CL-D1 | Channel | Entity | Domain entity representing a channel with visibility state |
| CL-D2 | AuditLogEntry | Entity | Single audit log record |
| CL-D3 | VisibilityChangeEvent | Event | Event emitted on visibility changes |
| CL-D4 | ChannelVisibility | Enumeration | Possible visibility states |
| CL-D5 | VisibilityUpdateRequest | DTO | Request payload for visibility update |
| CL-D6 | VisibilityUpdateResponse | DTO | Response payload for visibility update |
| CL-D7 | PublicChannelDTO | DTO | Public-facing channel data (see §4.6) |

### 4.6 PublicChannelDTO Fields

```typescript
interface PublicChannelDTO {
  id: string;           // Channel UUID
  name: string;         // Display name
  slug: string;         // URL-safe identifier
  topic: string;        // Channel topic/description
  messageCount: number; // Total messages in channel
  serverSlug: string;   // Parent server's slug
}
```

### 4.7 Rationale

Like the previous section, I had to reprompt to fix inconsistencies. The LLM also noticed that the ChannelRepository interface/class has discrepancies across each spec, so it consolidated each of them together. The class diagrams correctly display the interactions between each class, so no update was needed there. 

---

## 5. State Diagrams

### 5.1 System State Variables

| Variable | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| channel.visibility | ChannelVisibility | Current visibility state |
| channel.indexedAt | DateTime | Last sitemap inclusion timestamp |
| sitemap.lastModified | DateTime | Last sitemap update |
| auditLog.entries | AuditLogEntry[] | Audit records |

### 5.2 Channel Visibility State Machine

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> PRIVATE : Channel created (default)

    PRIVATE : PRIVATE\nindexedAt=null\nrobots="noindex, nofollow"
    PUBLIC_NO_INDEX : PUBLIC_NO_INDEX\nindexedAt=null\nrobots="noindex"
    PUBLIC_INDEXABLE : PUBLIC_INDEXABLE\nindexedAt=now()\nrobots="index, follow"

    PRIVATE --> PUBLIC_NO_INDEX : setVisibility(PUBLIC_NO_INDEX)\n[no side effects]
    PRIVATE --> PUBLIC_INDEXABLE : setVisibility(PUBLIC_INDEXABLE)\n[add to sitemap; notify search engines]

    PUBLIC_NO_INDEX --> PRIVATE : setVisibility(PRIVATE)\n[no side effects]
    PUBLIC_NO_INDEX --> PUBLIC_INDEXABLE : setVisibility(PUBLIC_INDEXABLE)\n[add to sitemap; notify search engines]

    PUBLIC_INDEXABLE --> PRIVATE : setVisibility(PRIVATE)\n[remove from sitemap; request de-index]
    PUBLIC_INDEXABLE --> PUBLIC_NO_INDEX : setVisibility(PUBLIC_NO_INDEX)\n[remove from sitemap; set noindex]
```

### 5.3 Admin Action State Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> A1 : Admin views channel

    A1 : A1: Settings Page Loaded\nisLoading=false
    A2 : A2: Confirmation Dialog\npendingVisibility=new
    A3 : A3: Updating\nisLoading=true
    A4 : A4: Error State\nerrorMessage=msg
    A5 : A5: Success State\nvisibility=updated

    A1 --> A2 : Admin clicks toggle
    A2 --> A1 : Cancel
    A2 --> A3 : Confirm
    A3 --> A4 : Error
    A3 --> A5 : Success
    A4 --> A1 : After 3s / dismiss
    A5 --> A1 : After 3s / dismiss
```

file.content:
### 6.1 Scenario: Admin Sets Channel to Public/Indexable

Admin navigates to channel settings and toggles a private channel to publicly indexable. System validates permissions, updates DB, regenerates sitemap, and notifies search engines.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Start(["START: Admin opens channel settings"])
    F11["F1.1 Load channel data\nChannelService.getChannel(channelId)"]
    F12["F1.2 Display current visibility toggle"]
    F13["F1.3 Show confirmation dialog"]
    Confirm{Admin confirms?}
    F15["F1.5 Cancel\nReturn to settings"]
    F16["F1.6 Send API request:\nupdateVisibility(channelId, PUBLIC_INDEXABLE)"]
    F17["F1.7 Validate JWT token"]
    TokenValid{Token valid?}
    Return401["Return 401"]
    F110["F1.10 Check admin permission"]
    HasPerm{Has permission?}
    Return403["Return 403"]
    F113["F1.13 Update visibility in DB"]
    F114["F1.14 Create audit log entry"]
    F115["F1.15 Emit VISIBILITY_CHANGED event"]
    F116["F1.16 Update sitemap"]
    F117["F1.17 Notify search engines (async)"]
    F118["F1.18 Invalidate cache"]
    F119["F1.19 Return success with updated channel"]
    SuccessResp{Success response?}
    F122["F1.22 Show error"]
    F123["F1.23 Update UI toggle"]
    End(["END: Channel is now public"])

    Start --> F11 --> F12 --> F13 --> Confirm
    Confirm -->|No| F15
    Confirm -->|Yes| F16 --> F17 --> TokenValid
    TokenValid -->|No| Return401
    TokenValid -->|Yes| F110 --> HasPerm
    HasPerm -->|No| Return403
    HasPerm -->|Yes| F113 --> F114 --> F115 --> F116 --> F117 --> F118 --> F119 --> SuccessResp
    SuccessResp -->|No| F122
    SuccessResp -->|Yes| F123 --> End
```

#### 6.1.1 Cross-Spec Integration: Visibility → PUBLIC_INDEXABLE

When visibility changes to `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE`:
1. Emit `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event via EventBus (Redis Pub/Sub)
2. **SEO Meta Tag Generation spec** consumes event → generates meta tags for the channel
3. **Guest Public Channel View spec** consumes event → warms guest view cache

### 6.2 Scenario: Anonymous User Views Public Channel

An anonymous user or crawler requests a public channel page. System verifies visibility and serves content with appropriate SEO headers.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Start(["START: Request to /c/{serverSlug}/{channelSlug}"])
    F21["F2.1 Route to PublicChannelController"]
    F22["F2.2 Check cache:\nchannel:{channelId}:visibility"]
    CacheHit{Cache hit?}
    F24["F2.4 Query DB"]
    F26["F2.6 Cache result"]
    F25["F2.5 Use cached value"]
    ChExists{Channel exists?}
    Return404["Return 404"]
    IsPublic{"Is PUBLIC_*?"}
    Return403["Return 403"]
    F211["F2.11 Fetch messages"]
    F212["F2.12 Set X-Robots-Tag header\nPUBLIC_INDEXABLE → index,follow\nPUBLIC_NO_INDEX → noindex"]
    F213["F2.13 Add canonical URL + structured data"]
